en.text: he is dear to Me
by the yoga of constant practice
attributeless aspect of Brahma
who has no attachment to hearth and home
who has given his mind and intelligence
of minds attached to the unmanifest
the way of attaining the unmanifest, the impersonal Brahma
and who is endowed with devotion
by the embodied soul
from elation, anger, fear, and grief
all the senses
Knowledge of Me
Giving up, for Me, the fruits of action
concentrate on offering your actions to Me
knowing Me as the supreme goal
who is contemplative or of controlled speech
in thought of Me, Śyāmasundar
dear to Me
in honour and dishonour
from the deathly ocean of material suffering
nor hankers for the unattained
there is no doubt
transcendental to the mundane
adore and follow
equipoised in unhappy and happy circumstances
sees with equality
to the welfare of all beings
give up, for Me, the fruits of all your actions
and a renouncer of all selfish undertakings
constantly with exclusive devotion
he is dear to Me
whose mind is peaceful
of these two types of yogīs (jñāna-yogī and bhakti-yogī)
and who considers abuse and praise equally
From giving up, for Me, the fruits of action
and who is endowed with devotion
is the best knower of yoga?
are the best knowers of yoga
a renouncer of both auspicious and inauspicious acts
and in the presence of cold, heat, pleasure, and pain

ru.text: непогрешимому, нетленному, непостижимому для чувств Брахману
в деятельности на благо всех существ
постоянно связаны с Тобой узами любви
чистый как внутренне, так и внешне
